title: Reference Counting, Weak References, and Cycles · Memory Trace: Retain Release Invariant
summary: A document graph shares nodes across views and leaks after parent-child cycles appear. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem retain release invariant increment in overflow-safe reference-counted graph. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Maintain a reference count equal to defined strong ownership edges and release exactly when the last edge ends.
Measure internal and external fragmentation, peak live bytes, reserved bytes, churn, and locality before redesign.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
